question: Is the overall basketball court environment rendered in a realistic photography style?
Does the dunking basketball player appear photorealistic (natural human anatomy, photographic skin/clothing texture, realistic shading)?
Is the basketball depicted as a glowing neon tube-style sphere (bright emissive glow, tubular neon appearance)?
Is the defending figure rendered as a silhouette consistent with traditional Chinese shadow puppet style (flat cutout look, distinct puppet-like contouring)?
Has the backboard transformed into a stained glass window style, featuring visible lead lines and colored glass segments?
Does the stained glass backboard feature religious iconography (e.g., haloed figures, ecclesiastical motifs)?
Are tactical play diagrams visible on the court floor rendered in a chalk drawing style (chalk-like lines, dusty texture)?
Are the spectators in the stands rendered as Easter Island moai statues, exhibiting stone material texture and monolithic features?
Is there clear style differentiation between the photorealistic dunking player and the shadow-puppet silhouette defender?
Are multiple distinct art styles (realistic, neon, shadow puppet, stained glass, chalk, stone statue) visibly coexisting in the same image without collapsing into a single unified style?
